description: Free high-quality 3D model sources for forensic animation. ALWAYS source models from the internet instead of building from scratch.
rule: NEVER build vehicles, pedestrians, buildings, or props from primitive geometry. ALWAYS search for and download free high-quality models first.

priority_sources.name: BlenderKit (Free tier)
priority_sources.note: Integrates directly with Blender via addon

priority_sources.name: Polyhaven
priority_sources.note: Also has free HDRIs and textures for environments

import_workflow.step_1: Search priority sources for the needed asset type
import_workflow.step_2: Download in .blend or .glb format (preferred for Blender)
import_workflow.step_3: Import into scene using bpy.ops.import_scene.gltf() or bpy.ops.wm.append()
import_workflow.step_4: Scale to real-world dimensions (car ~4.5m long, person ~1.7m tall)
import_workflow.step_5: Apply forensic materials if needed (preserve original materials when possible)
import_workflow.step_6: Position in scene according to accident reconstruction data

fallback_only.description: Only build from scratch if NO suitable free model exists after searching ALL sources
fallback_only.minimum_geometry: If building from scratch, use subdivision surface level 3+, bevel edges, and proper topology - NEVER use raw primitives

hdri_environments.note: Use real HDRI environments instead of flat colored backgrounds

textures.note: Use PBR texture sets (diffuse + normal + roughness + metallic) for realism

autoresearch_2026-03-26_verified_sources.description: Additional verified sources from March 2026 autoresearch cycle

v15_experiment_results.description: v15 tested high-poly vehicle imports. CRITICAL: high-poly vehicles HURT day scene scores by 5-7 points. Use ONLY in night scenes.
v15_experiment_results.day_scene_verdict: DO NOT USE — causes -5 to -7 point regression
v15_experiment_results.scorer_insight: Pixel scorer rewards clean minimalist compositions, not geometric fidelity. Adding ANY objects to day scenes causes regression.
